title: Toilet in Passenger accommodation
description: The door lock of passenger women toilet not able to lock properly
correctiveAction: Informed cengr. to check the door lock and if possible to replace a new one,

title: Toilet vaccum system
description: The toilet vaccum system keep running even not using,
correctiveAction: Advised Cengr to check all toilet if there  is air leaking in the hose or pipings. after few minutes of searching the toilet in the cabin of deck boy and stewardess found broken hoseclip caused of air leaking cengr immediately look for new hose clip for replacement

title: explosion proof portable radio
description: the issued portable radio for firemans outfit are not explosion proof and the Class surveyor required to have 2 unit onboard
correctiveAction: informed the ship owner to purchase a new portable radio that marked with explosion proof and aproved by solas

title: anti splash tape for auxilliary engine
description: During Rina class survey the inspector found out that the top of auxilliary engine in injector pipings not properly covered by anti splash tape,
correctiveAction: Right away the engr. went to the store to purchase the said tape that approved by solas

title: Busted lights in bridge stairway
description: lights at stairway going to the bridge was busted and dark on the stairs way up
correctiveAction: Captain adviced the crew to buy fourescent to the store because out of stock  spare parts onboard, and remind the crew to have a regular inventory of spare parts.
finalManagementComment: NC closed and lightbulb is changed.

title: Cleaning snow on deck
description: Crew working on deck, clearing snow using shovel, the temperature so cooled and not using proper clothes or Personal protective equiptment, I noticed the gloves and shoes was not suitable for cold weather, and might be suffer from frostbite
correctiveAction: Immediately  stopped him and invite him inside and adviced to change the proper clothes to avoid exposed from cold weather.
finalManagementComment: Master to ensure that crew have and use the proper clothing.

title: Battery of Generator no.1
description: During testing of Generator no. 1, Mechanic failed to start the generator, and he found out the batteries was not giving enough volts
correctiveAction: We advised the owner to purchase 2 units of batteries for replacement
finalManagementComment: Corrective actions accepted. 2 new batteries received.

title: Radio Batteries
description: During test we found out the batteries not giving enough Volts
correctiveAction: requested electrician to check the batteries and to replace new one if necessary
finalManagementComment: Corrective actions accepted. 2 new batteries replaced.

title: expired equiptment onboard
description: During normal routine we found out pyrotechnics, mob smoke signals and epirb HRU expired
correctiveAction: informed the company to purchase the said equiptment as soon as possible
finalManagementComment: Corrective actions accepted. Case will be closed once delivered. Replaced new Rockets, Hru, MOB smoke signal

title: Both fireman's outfits kept together in one place - not wide separated
description: During Rina Inspection he found out 2 sets of Fireman outsfits placed together, because 1 set just arrived on board newly purchased
correctiveAction: Adviced crew to find  other location on 2nd deck accomodation it should be closed to the emergency light and provide box for keeping one set of fireman's outfit
finalManagementComment: Corrective actions accepted. Built new fireman's outfit locker located on 2nd deck accomodation

title: Draught stop missing in over a ceiling panels om maindeck to be provided, space lenght 14 meters
description: During Rina inspection on board  he found out no draught installed in the passenger acommodation ceiling
correctiveAction: Advised crew to open the ceiling and to install draught in every 14 meters lenght from the bulkhead
finalManagementComment: Corrective actions accepted. Installed in pax accomodtion as per Rina instruction

title: Emergency lightning at stowage position of liferafts and rescue boat to be provided
description: Only one emergency lights installed in the vicinity the Rina inspector required two units 1 focus to the  liferaft stowage and 1 focus to the water lunching area
correctiveAction: advised the owner to deliver 3 units of emergency lights to install as soon as possible on board
finalManagementComment: Corrective actions accepted. Case closed once installed.

title: Poster with operating instruction for liferafts in theier vicinity
description: During Rina inspection found out  no liferaft instruction posted in the vicinity, the old one removed during painting,
correctiveAction: Advised crew to post new instruction procedure in front of liferaft port and starboard

title: Overdue internal audit
description: An overdue internal audit is cause by the vessel not being in operation.
correctiveAction: Audit scheduled on 2025.09.03
finalManagementComment: Audit completed. Report attached to the system.

title: clogging of bridge window sprinkler
description: no water come out from sprinkler head to wash the window glass in  the bridge
correctiveAction: advised the crew to dismantle the sprinkler head and pipings to remove the clogging
finalManagementComment: New pipe was installed. NC closed.

title: sprinkler onboard
description: During port state inspection they found out the boat still no sprinkler system installed and they issued detainable defficiency to the boat
correctiveAction: Informed the DPA, owner, Rina Class, and submitted all reports, still ongoing discussion about the regulation, also planning to install sprinkler system onboard
finalManagementComment: PAX number reduced to 36. No need sprinkler system.

title: Co2 Room Not Gas tight door
description: During port state inspection they found that the co2 room was not gas tight door and issued detenable defficiency for the boat
correctiveAction: Informed DP, owner, Rina class, still in discussion but planning to move outside accomodation and to make new cabinet for the Co2
finalManagementComment: Case closed. New fire plan issued.

title: MOB crane motor defective
description: While  operating MOB boat for monthly Test suddenly the motor stopped and and no power and not able to move the crane
correctiveAction: informed cengr to check the problem and we found out the motor was defective and and not working anymore, we find the spare motor for crane and replaced with new one.

title: working in enclosed space
description: planning to work inside enclosed space inside pontoon, without proper ventillation, for painting
correctiveAction: I advised the crew to provide ventillation  in the area, and should 've be wore gas mask before they start working inside the pontoon.
finalManagementComment: Procedure for working inside enclosed spaces to be followed.

title: search light starboard
description: while testing all light switches in bridge panel board, we found out that the search light stbd not working
correctiveAction: we checked  and open the cover and foud out the bulb were busted, we informed  the shorebase electrician onboard to order new bulb

title: crane hydraulic pipe leakage
description: While operating the crane here in drydock suddenly one pipe was broken that caused of oil leaking, we foind out that the pipe was rusty and old
correctiveAction: we checked and dismatled the pipe by the help of shipyard worker to replace the said pipe

title: busted light in stairs going up to Bridge
description: Lights in the stairs toward bridge was busted and  lack of light during night time
correctiveAction: advice the enginners to replace the busted bulb or flourescent to avoid any accident while walking in the stairs
finalManagementComment: Light was fixed by the engineer.

title: Passenger stumbled on deck
description: while the boat sailing for fjordcruise we encountered heavy swell and passenger stubled on deck and hitted the left ear to the side of the stairs
correctiveAction: adviced the crew to give first aid to the 1 guest suffered of mild injury, clean the wound and covered with band.aid advised the passengers not to stand in the front or aft deck of the boat or outside  if the sea condition is roughsea and heavy swell
finalManagementComment: We suggest to put no go areas signs, ropes when vessel is sailing in rough waters.

title: Steering indicator not working well
description: The steering indicator in the bridge not functiong to Port side only going to starboard, but the rudder of the boat still working only the indicator was the problem,
correctiveAction: Engrs onboard checked the steering room to make sure the rudder is moving in both direction, adviced maintenance personnel of the henningsen about the situation and to check the sensor
managementComment: Maintenance to be done when vessel is back in Tromso, due to lack of electricians in Longyearbean

title: Ships pontoon dent
description: Whie sailing here in svalbard the whole fjord covered with ice, and the boat obligue to break the ice everyday, to cross the fjord before we reach our destination, sometimes there is some part of ice that very hard and caused of dent to portside pontoon forward part 1 dent only around 100 cm length
correctiveAction: when we noticed the dent we immediately opened the pontoon manhole cover and check inside, luckily no sign of crack or damage inside and no sign of water leaking The dent part was along the forward manhole of pontton , please check photo attached
finalManagementComment: The dent will be fixed in the drydock.

title: toilet vaccum
description: suddenly the pressure of toilet vaccum disappeard and no flush in the toilet
correctiveAction: cengr and crew checked all toilet bowl mechanism and pipings all the way down to engine room, if there is any  leak and clogging to the system
managementComment: The system was clogged - was removed.

title: Generator no. 1 unusual sound/noise during running
description: While using the no 1 generator suddenlly the sound changed and C/e suspecting the injection pump not working well
correctiveAction: advised the company maintenace personnel about the observation, C/e planning to open the generator to check the problem
managementComment: Fan belt tighmeted - everything ok.

title: Main engine lubeoil temperature
description: While underway to tromso the M/E lubeoil temperature rising and pressure decreasing
correctiveAction: we reduced the speed of M/E to avoid overheating of lubeoil pressure, and adviced the company maintenance personnel about the observation.
managementComment: Oil changed, and filter replaced, problem solved.

title: Navigational lights busted
description: Alarm at bridge panel board, indicating that the port side sidelights was busted
correctiveAction: Advised the crew to replace immediately with new spare bulb
managementComment: Replaced with new light bulbs

title: hydraulic hose
description: During crane inspection before we start crane operation we found out 1 hydraulic hose was damaged
correctiveAction: advised the crew to removed the damaged hose and bring to company repair shop to get new spare.
managementComment: Hose replaced - case closed

title: Galley refrigerator
description: Refrigerator in galley not working well and noisy sound of motor, and also not giving right temperature.
correctiveAction: advised the office to purchase new unit of refrigerator for replacement.
managementComment: Refrigirator replaced - closed.

title: Storage capacity for waste on board
description: storage capacity inadequate for next voyage
correctiveAction: Other sludge to be transferred in drums until we reach 75% available capacity and we stored ashore/office
managementComment: Waste oli delivered ashore in LBY - case closed..

title: Pre- arrival notification to current port
description: All categories of Garabage not reported in SSN
correctiveAction: Waste disposal to be reported in SSN system as soon as possible
managementComment: Were the Master instructed by the inspector to report arrival/departures in SafeSeaNet when the vessel is operating on day trips in Svalbard? If so we will set this up. Please follow up..
finalManagementComment: Re-arrival waste declared in SafeSeaNet - will be done each year when leaving Tromsø.

title: Garbage Record books WR
description: Garbage categories to be listed for each category in the garbage record book when delivered to PRF
correctiveAction: followed the instruction as per garbage management plan regulation
managementComment: Corrective action accepted.

title: L.O. alarm in Engine room
description: suddenly the L.O lights in engine room and bridge panel alarmed,
correctiveAction: Cengr, checked the L.O. filter and he found out that the filter was clogged with dirt, immediately replaced new one and clean the central cooler

title: Broken hydraulic hose of cargo crane
description: During crane operation suddenly 1 hydraulic hose broken and oil scattered on deck
correctiveAction: Immediately stopped the power of crane, and crew diconnected the broken hose and sent ashore to get new one for replacement
finalManagementComment: Please inspect all hoses o the cargo crane to learn whether other hoses need replacing.

title: Life bouy in Main deck - PSC Deficiency #2
description: 11117 - Lifebuoys incl. provision and disposition - not as required. The lifebuoy placed in the main deck was not according to Solas standards, they were too heavy, weighing almost 4 kg.
correctiveAction: 3 pcs. new lifebuoys were ordered weighing  2.5 kgs as per Solas regulation.
managementComment: Lifebuoys delivered on board.

title: Fjordcruise passage plan - PSC deficiency #1
description: 10127 - Voyage or passage plan - Incomplete. The passage plan installed in C-map was not applicable to use because the C-map onboard was not approved by Polar code
correctiveAction: A new passage plan on checklists 7-0105A and B was prepared immediately, using chart 523.
managementComment: Passage- and Voyage-plans to be prepared according to procedures.
finalManagementComment: Closed out - will be followed up at internal audit.

title: Busted masthead light
description: The navigation lights panel alarm sounds in the bridge panel board. Masthead light broken.
correctiveAction: To be changed immediately
managementComment: Noted

title: Busted Navigational lights starboard
description: The alarm goes in the bridge panel board navigational lights, which means one of the navigation lights was busted
correctiveAction: we replaced immediately the 220 bulb of the green sidelights, starboard side
managementComment: Noted.

title: generator break down
description: While the ship was underway Chief engineer heard an unusual sound from the generator, then after 10 minutes the engine/generator suddenly stopped
correctiveAction: Generator number 1 started automatically. Upon arrival in port, C/E checked the problem, he found that the gear and bearing of the oil pump were damaged, we informed the company to order a new spare
managementComment: The oil pump was replaced with a new one.

title: Battery and alternator of Generator no. 1
description: Battery low voltage supply and not giving enough power to start the engine, alternator giving over-voltage when starting the engine after the battery has been charged
correctiveAction: To replace the battery and check the alternator of generator no. 1
managementComment: The battery was replaced, and the voltage of the alternator was checked, all ok.

title: Slippery Deck
description: During Embarking of the guest the main entrance  deck was covered with ice and slippery, and the guest almost fell on the deck
correctiveAction: Capt. advised the crew to lay down a mat in the entrance and apply sand to the deck and rock salt, and post a sign of a slippery deck
managementComment: If this situation reoccurs, a procedure is to be implemented.

title: not wearing hardhat during heaving  up anchor chain in drydock
description: AB forgot to wear hard hat while lowering anchor chain in shipyard while the boat in drydock
correctiveAction: Advised to stop the operation and to get the hard hat first and wear it before commencing the job.
managementComment: Toolbox meetings to be held before starting critical jobs.

title: self closing valve on sounding pipes in engine room
description: The self-closing valve was installed in the wrong direction to be  cleared the obstruction
correctiveAction: Advised the chief engr, to adjust the self-closing valve pipe, to give space to the self-closing valve to close properly
managementComment: Pipe and valve refitted.

title: Froozen roller fairlead in forcastle deck
description: Stuck up due to the boat laid up for almost 5 months Not maintained before going into operation.
correctiveAction: Roller to be removed from the base and to clean inside and apply grease
managementComment: Roller maintained

title: External ISM Audit - NC 2 - Risk Assessments
description: There are issued Risk Assessments for some critical operations, like Crane wire greasing, Picking up people from ice, Applying Rust remover etc. At the time of the audit, an insufficient number of Risk Assessments were found onboard. Several critical operations were lacking effective Risk Assessment, like Working in height, Mooring, Anchoring, Manoeuvring in harsh Weather etc.
correctiveAction: Risk assessments uploaded - due to system failure these were not visible to the crew.
managementComment: Further risk assessments will be elaborated in the near future.

title: External ISM Audit - NC 1 - Ineffective maintenace
description: Signs of ineffective maintenance schedule implementation. Objective evidence is Fuel leaking from the Auxiliary Engine, minor leaking from quick closing valves hydraulics, all outdoor lights on the boat deck missing their weather integrity due to missing lids or damaged lamp covers, and two life buoys missing port of registry.
correctiveAction: All finding dealt with as soon as practically possible. Regarding ligths on boat deck covers are not available any more, new lamps have been ordered, waiting for delivery. Home port painted on life buoys
managementComment: All items will be closed at drydock in February 2023

title: starboard side search light Busted
description: Busted bulb need replacement/ for the meantime we can use port side searchlight
correctiveAction: Removed the busted bulb and used for sample, Planning to buy new spare here in tromso
managementComment: Bulb replaced.

title: Leaking in AUX engine and minor leaking from quick closing valve
description: Caused by leaking, damaged oil seal or gasket The boat is sailing every day and there's no time to fix Minor leaking and still  in operational condition
correctiveAction: Priority in the Job order list, and to be fixed once the boat has done sailing in fjord cruise this season, and the boat to be drydocked on September.
managementComment: Noted - to be closed when done.

title: All outdoors light on boat deck missing its weather integrity due to missing lids or damaged lamps cover
description: Very old kind of out door lights and you cant buy spare in store
correctiveAction: Included in a job order list and to be replaced new sets of out door light in September while the boat in shipyard
managementComment: Noted - to be closed when rectified.

title: Missing text in life bouy port of registry
description: old sticker and  no adhesive any more
correctiveAction: Re wrote new text in lifebouys port of registry

title: Deteriorated hydraulic hoses on mc gregor system.
description: During inspection we found out the hoses of mc gregor not in good looking and started deteriorating and sooner or later cause of leakage
correctiveAction: Chief Engineer disconnected the old hoses of McGregor and sent them ashore, to replace a new one.
managementComment: Hose(s) replaced.

title: A passenger stumbled on forcastle deck.
description: A passenger was during the Fjordcruise standing and standby at forecastle deck during sightseeing, and accidentally while she is walking she is not looking in her step and her foot hit the bitts in the front and she is stumbled on deck. luckily she is fine and not hurt so much.
correctiveAction: We assisted the woman and gave her ice to apply on her foot, and I advised our guide onboard to include during a briefing in the morning to give extra care while walking on deck.

title: Main engine No. 4 injector low exhaust pressure
description: Chief Engineer noticed that the exhaust pressure for no 4 injectors was decreasing.
correctiveAction: Chief Engineer checked the exhaust pressure regularly to observe the condition of the injector and prepared the new  spare injector to conduct a pressure test before installing the parts to make sure it is working well
managementComment: Injector replaced.

title: lLubricating oil pump is leaking.
description: A leakage occurred during preparation to start the main engine
correctiveAction: Chief Engineer checked the cause of the leaking and replaced the old pump with new spare pump

title: Ships whistle not working
description: Totally no sounds the ships whistle even it is supplied sufficient air pressure
correctiveAction: Captain and Deck boy went up to monkey island  to checked  the ship whistle parts, clean the solenoid, unit, and clean the pipe that full of dirt  by releasing strong pressure air from the engine room.,

title: Galley sink was clogged
description: The sink in galley was totally clogged and the cook complaining because the water stocked and she cant drain
correctiveAction: informed Chief Engr. to work out the problem, we traced the pipe down to copperdom  connected to sink in the  galley, and we disconnected. we found out that full of dirt inside the pipe and blocked the passage.

title: General alarm sound error
description: The general alarm for abandonship sounds error, supposed to be 7 short blast followed by 1 long blast but now 3hort  blast or sometimes 4 short blast the alarm.
correctiveAction: We called the electrician from JM Hansen, he checked the fire alarm system, but he need to know first the program from the manufacturer, but he called already and were waiting to send the program
managementComment: Repaired - system now working correctly.

title: Obstruction in cargo hold stairs
description: Empty cartons /boxes  and ropes filed along the stairs in cargo hold and obstructed the way.
correctiveAction: Advised the deck boy to clean and dispose all empty boxes to the garbage and secure the mooring rope away from the stairway.

title: Passenger sitting in ships bulwark
description: A passenger sitting in bulwark for taking a photo in the front of the boat and it might be dangerous and fall overboard
correctiveAction: The crew approached the passenger and told the passenger to go down and not to sit on top of bulwark

title: Earth fault in fire alarm system
description: Alarm in fire alarm panel and showing Earth fault to the system
correctiveAction: We informed the office HTG to send elctrician onboard to check the problem
managementComment: Rectified

title: icy fore deck
description: Because of cold conditions in our area of operation sometimes the deck of the ship is icy and slippery and passengers almost fell down on the deck
correctiveAction: I advised the chief officer and deck boy to check regularly the deck and if possible scrape the ice or put sand on the top of the ice
managementComment: Noted

title: Lashing of chairs in sundeck during helicopter training exercise
description: During the helicopter exercise onboard the plastic  chair on sundeck flew away toward to the passenger because of the propeller wind of helicopter
correctiveAction: Adviced the crew that before we conduct helicopter exercise we need  to secure all chair with lashing in sundeck
managementComment: Noted.

title: The gangway safety net was damaged
description: The safety net attached in gangway was torn and damaged while using in docking in barentsburg port
correctiveAction: For the mean time we removed the net and were trying to fix the damaged part before we put it back
managementComment: Noted.

title: MOB engine hard to start
description: Because of cold climate in area of ship operation the engine exposed in a cold, (svalbard)
correctiveAction: I advised the crew to cover the engine with a thick clothes and doubled by tarpauline to maintaine warm inside the motor
managementComment: Noted - good input.

title: Air compressor - oil level level.
description: Air compressor oil level was low, and almost empty in the tank.
correctiveAction: We ordered oil immediately ashore, and we used the thinnest hydraulic oil onboard while waiting for the supply to avoid overheating the motor.
finalManagementComment: Rectified - and closed. Engineer to monitor the oil level at appropriate intervals.

title: Passenger fell down on the floor
description: Passenger accidentally fell down in passenger accommodation, caused by a wet floor.
correctiveAction: The crew dried the floor and placed anti slip door mat on the floor
managementComment: COntent duly noted. Crew must ensure that floors are kept dry.

title: Crane wire damaged
description: One strand of crane wire damage  closed to the hook and to be fixed ashore
correctiveAction: The owner arranged machine shop to checked and  fixed the damage wire today or tomorrow before the crane back to operation
managementComment: Noted - will be closed when done.

title: rudder indicator
description: no lights in rudder indicator and hard to see during night time.
correctiveAction: we opened the panel board and check inside, the small bulb busted and we changed new spare bulb.

title: Forward floodlight portside not working
description: when i switched the floodlight it is not working, and we tried to open and checked the bulb we found out it is busted
correctiveAction: we replaced a new one right away  and i  ordered a new spare

title: Gangway rope line twisted
description: While loweing the passenger Gangway, suddenly the rope twisted and stocked the rope in pulley.
correctiveAction: we removed the shackle connected to pulley to loosen the rope and to  remove the tangled inside the pulley.

title: leakage in fire hose nozzle
description: during drill we found out there is a leaking on firehose nozzle
correctiveAction: after the drill we checked the leaking and we fixed the gasket inside the fire hose nozzle.

title: MOB boat hard to start
description: we lunched MOB boat for weekly inspection but we failed to start.
correctiveAction: we brought back the MOB on deck and we removed the motor  to check if their is something problem to the engine, and we found out the gasoline tank mixed with rain water, we immediately replace the gasoline inside the tank.
managementComment: Corrective actions accepted. How the water came into the tank, from which place?

title: Main engine Push rod oil  leakage
description: oil Leakage came out from the base of M/E  push rod #3-4-5-6, Ce tried to tightening the nuts and bolts but still leaking,
correctiveAction: We decided to pull out all 4 M/E push rod to change the O ring and gasket to stop the leak.
managementComment: Please inform ISM when evaluated.

title: Main engine clutch alarm
description: alarm showed up in the bridge and engine panel board if the boat slowing down and stop during manuevering clutch appeared in the alarm.
correctiveAction: C/E checked and clean the filter of of clutch because it is totally clogged and dirty, I advised to clean the filter regularly and request new spare of filter to the said parts.

title: Restricted area signage in bridge deck
description: Restricted area signage was faded and  not clear for the passenger  to read, so that they climbed directly to the bridge deck even the radar is ON and scanner is rotating. it might be harm the person if hit of the rotating scanner.
correctiveAction: i advised my crew to  put a new signage/placard with chain or rope along the stairs  to prevent passage to the bridge deck
finalManagementComment: Well done, please inspect all areas with all signs and replace if necessary. Case closed.

title: navigational lights busted
description: bridge navigational lights panel received alarm, detected stern light not working.
correctiveAction: Opened  the lights cover and checked the bulb and replaced an new bulb.

title: Hand sanitizer again after sight seeing tour in Russian settlement, barentsburg/Pyramiden
description: All the passenger in the morning before enter in the boat they sanitized their hands, but in the other port after in the russian settlement  after tour, the guide and crew forgot to sanitized again the hands of all passenger.
correctiveAction: Reminded  the guide and crew not to forget to sanitized again all passenger after tour in the russian settlement to make sure the boat free from viruses.

title: children climbing in anchor windlass
description: while the boat sailing for fjord cruise trip the passengers/kids suddenly climbed and play on the top of  anchor windlass in the bow
correctiveAction: one crew went downstairs and advised the parents of the kid, dont let their kids play or climb on the top of windlass because there is chance to fell down and hurts the kids. advised the guide to include in the briefing before departure if there is a kid onboard
finalManagementComment: Corrective actions accepted. Restricted area posters should be in place.

title: Toilet vacuum motor not working
description: toilet vacuum motor not working anymore it might be burn inside or reached the required running hours.
correctiveAction: advised the company to buy  new motor to replace the broken motor onboard as soon as possible.
managementComment: Corrective actions accepted. Please inform when evaluated.
finalManagementComment: Case closed as Captain informed that the case is fixed.

title: bilge sensor alarm in steering room
description: there is a small amount of water in the bilge of steering room, and during rough sea condition the water is moving and hit the sensor
correctiveAction: chief engr. and chief officer removed and empty the water in steering room bilge .
finalManagementComment: Corrective actions accepted. Good job. Case closed 20.06.2020

title: water tight door plate deteriorated
description: some doors plating are deteriorated and need for replacement
correctiveAction: i advised to cut and removed the deteriorated part of the water tight door and change a new plate
finalManagementComment: Corrective action accepted. Well done. Case closed.20.06.2020

title: wet deck in sundeck and maindeck
description: after boat tour crew washing the entire accommodation with  fresh water using the hose, there is some part the water stocked in the floor and the floor still wet  and there is a chance the guest slip and stumble.
correctiveAction: i advised the crew that after washing the deck, mop and dry the  deck,

title: social distancing in the Bar
description: some of the guest while in a queue at the bar they are very closed to each other
correctiveAction: Advised the stewardess and the guide to inform the passenger during briefing about social distancing onboard,  marked  the floor  with signed about 1 meter distance for queue purposes

title: Clogged toilet pipe
description: one of the crew disposed and flushed tissue paper in toilet bowl that caused of clogging into the pipe
correctiveAction: advised all crew to dispose tissue paper in trash bin not  on toilet bowl,advised  Engr and crew to work together to unclog, toilet bowl to be lifted to clean the pipe and to remove the clogged

title: Hull dented port and starboard Pontoon
description: The boat sailing and meet so many hard drifted ice we tried to avoid by slow speed but still hitting the forward part of pontoon that caused of dented to the hull
correctiveAction: we conducted inspection from outside and re- open the tank of on forward part of the pontoon port and stbd no sign of hole. Deffeciency also issued by Norwegian maritime  authority #20.and to be assisted by Class RINA.
managementComment: Corrective actions accepted. We will await information from NMA and class.
finalManagementComment: Case closed after video call with NMA May 17.

title: ships whistle not working
description: the ships whistle not working when started during drill even enough air in the tank.
correctiveAction: we opened the ships whistle box located in bridge deck and clean the inside and spray with cleaning chemical, advised C/O and C/E to conduct regular check up of ships whistle weekly.
managementComment: Is it fixed now?

title: cooler strainer full of ice
description: while sailing in cold temperature below zero and the sea forming ice the suction for cooling system sucked ice and clogged to the strainer that caused of rising the temperature of main engine /generator and lube oil pressure
correctiveAction: advised chief engr. that if he noticed the temperature started rising open the other suction and clean the strainer to removed the ice and make sure the valve well closed.

title: additional mooring rope during bad weather
description: suddenly the weather became bad while moored at the pier and the ship moving a lot up and down and there is a possibility to break the rope because of strong impact
correctiveAction: advice the deck officer to check the weather for cast everyday and make sure to put additional mooring rope while the boat lying in the pier mostly if the weather getting bad.

title: radar scanner rotating/open
description: one of the crew went up to the bridge deck while the scanner is switch on and rotating, it might be caused of radiation.or accidentally hit the head.
correctiveAction: advised all crew to ask permission to the bridge before they climb up to the bridge deck and make sure the radar scanner is switch off and not rotating.
finalManagementComment: Corrective action accepted. You could print and put on the windows poster that before going up to the bridge deck, please inform the bridge.

title: Bridge door signage/restricted
description: The passenger in and out to the bridge during boat tour, and the navigating officer expose and close contact to the guest in a limited space that might be infected by corona
correctiveAction: Printed and posted sign to the bridge door entrance showing restricted area, and advised the guide to mention during briefing before departure
finalManagementComment: Well done. Rules should be strict now and no any passengers allowed to pass restricted areas.

title: Clean and disinfect the Passenger accommodation
description: to prevent the infection from COVID 19 for the crew and other passenger
correctiveAction: Posted poster for preventing virus, clean with disinfectant all knobs,railings, tables and all surfaces expose for the passenger, after the trip and advised the passenger to wash their hands regularly.
finalManagementComment: Well done. This is serious nowadays so we all need to be careful.

title: Main engine high Pressure pipe leakage
description: while engine is running the high pressure pipe connected to injector was leakage,
correctiveAction: when the boat arrived in port we replaced a new spare, we found out that the pipe has cracked cause of leaking
finalManagementComment: Corrective actions accepted. Well done. Case closed. 15.03.2020

title: toilet vacuum pump sensor not working properly
description: the vacuum pump motor not automatically stop after used and motor continues running,
correctiveAction: checked the sensor and removed the clogged of piping connected to sensor

title: Slippery deck
description: Because of winter season and unpredictable weather sometimes snowing, windy, rough sea and sea spray on deck passenger prone to stumble because of slippery deck

title: ISM Audit REC #1 - Training Manuals
description: t is recommended that SOLAS training manuals, covering both Life-Saving Appliances (LSA) and Fire Fighting (FF), be obtained and made available onboard. The provision of these manuals would support structured training and familiarization, thereby enhancing overall safety standards on board. This is particularly important given the high turnover of crew members, as consistent access to up-to-date training materials will help ensure that all personnel are adequately informed, properly trained, and capable of responding effectively in emergency situations.
correctiveAction: Issue discussed with the Master.

title: ISM Audit NC #5 - Deck crane not inspedted
description: During the audit, it was noted that no valid certificate could be produced to confirm that the deck crane had undergone its required annual inspection. In accordance with applicable regulations and industry standards, deck cranes must be inspected and certified by an authorized and competent company at least once every 12 months. The absence of such documentation raises concerns regarding both regulatory compliance and the safe operation of the equipment, as regular inspections are essential to verify the crane’s structural integrity, operational reliability, and overall safety.
correctiveAction: The master instructed to arrange inspection of the deck crane as soon as possible.

title: ISM Audit OBS #5 - Sterring Gear pumps
description: During the audit, it was identified that the hydraulic power unit for the steering gear is equipped with only a single hydraulic pump. This configuration presents a potential risk, as the failure of the sole pump could result in a loss of steering capability, thereby creating a hazardous situation for the vessel, particularly during critical operations such as maneuvering in confined waters or adverse conditions. It is therefore recommended that consideration be given to installing a second, independent hydraulic pump in order to provide redundancy. Such an arrangement would enhance the overall reliability of the steering system, improve operational safety, and ensure continued manoeuvrability in the event of equipment failure.
correctiveAction: N/A

title: ISM Audit OBS #4 - No UKC specified
description: During the audit, it was identified that the required Under Keel Clearance (UKC) was neither defined in the Master’s Standing Orders nor addressed within the vessel’s procedures for passage planning. The absence of clearly specified UKC criteria may lead to inconsistencies in voyage planning and execution, and does not align with best practice for ensuring safe navigation, particularly in confined or shallow waters.
correctiveAction: Master instructed to rectify

title: ISM Audit OBS #3 - Standing Orders
description: During the audit, it was established that the Master’s Standing Orders had not been signed by all navigation officers, and that the Chief Engineer’s Standing Orders were not available onboard.
correctiveAction: Master instructed to rectify before departure.

title: ISM Audit OBS # 2 - Risk Assessments.
description: During the audit, it was observed that only a limited number of risk assessments were available onboard. In accordance with the requirements of the International Safety Management (ISM) Code, risk assessments must be systematically conducted for all operations and tasks that may pose a potential hazard to the vessel, its crew, or the environment. The absence of sufficient documented risk assessments indicates that this requirement is not being fully implemented, and it raises concerns regarding the vessel’s ability to adequately identify, evaluate, and mitigate operational risks.
correctiveAction: The Master is instructed to ensure that risk assessments are conducted in full compliance with the requirements of the applicable code.

title: ISM Audit OBS #1 - Nautical publicatios outdated.
description: During the audit, it was identified that the IAMSAR Manual onboard was the 2022 edition. Nautical publications are required to be maintained in their latest available editions, and therefore this does not meet the applicable requirements.
correctiveAction: The Master instructed to replace IAMSAR as soon as possible.

title: ISM Audit NC #4 - Material Data SHeets
description: During the audit, a number of chemical substances were identified onboard; however, the corresponding Material Safety Data Sheets (MSDS) could not be produced upon request. This indicates that the vessel does not maintain adequate documentation for hazardous materials, as required, and raises concerns regarding the crew’s ability to access critical safety information related to handling, storage, and emergency response procedures for these substances.
correctiveAction: The Master was instructed to review and rationalize all chemicals onboard, ensuring that only approved substances are retained and that each is accompanied by the appropriate Material Safety Data Sheet (MSDS).

title: ISM Audit NC #3 - Emergency Drills
description: During the audit, it was determined that not all crew members had participated in emergency drills within the past month, meaning the vessel is not in compliance with SOLAS regulations. Engineers and Ordinary Seamen are engaged on a trip-by-trip basis rather than on a fixed schedule, resulting in the vessel departing port with approximately two-thirds of the crew having not taken part in a drill during the preceding month.
correctiveAction: The Master is instructed not to depart from port before all crewmembers has participated i an emergency drill.

title: ISM Audit NC #2 - Immersion suits not inspected
description: During the onboard inspection, it was identified that the immersion suits had not been inspected and maintained in accordance with the requirements of SOLAS regulations. Regular inspection of immersion suits is essential to ensure that they remain in good condition, are free from defects, and are fully operational in the event of an emergency. The absence of proper inspection records or evidence of routine checks raises concerns regarding the readiness and reliability of this critical life-saving equipment, and indicates non-compliance with the applicable safety requirements.
correctiveAction: The Master was instructed to arrange inspection of the immersion suits as soon as practically.

title: ISM Audit NC #1 - Expired certifikates
description: During the ISM/MLC audit conducted onboard, it was identified that several certificates contained within the vessel’s certificate file had expired and were no longer valid. Maintaining up-to-date statutory and class certificates is a fundamental requirement to ensure compliance with applicable regulations and to demonstrate the vessel’s continued fitness for operation. The presence of expired certificates indicates a deficiency in the vessel’s document control and monitoring procedures and may expose the vessel to regulatory non-compliance, potential detention, or operational restrictions.
correctiveAction: Master instructed to replace certificates in the folder immidiately.

title: Broken leg
description: Finnbogi Hansen rushed onboard Samson to get a bottle of water. He was at that time sailing Skúmarin, one of MEST's boats. A man from MEST Shipyard had some job to do in the cofferdam, but had left to pick up something. It was dark in the corridor, and Finnbogi did not see that the hatch was missing. The door we usually do use when entering the vessel, was locked.
correctiveAction: The emergency number 112 was dialed, and both police and ambulance came to rescue. The CEO and the HSE leader were informed. Now we have got barriers (red/white chains) ready to put up. One in front of the hatch, and one behind the hatch. Furthermore we have now a big yellow sign with black letters on the hatch where it is written: WARNING! PUT UP THE BARRIERS BEFORE REMOWING HATCH!
managementComment: Corrective action accepted. Please send a proposal for a procedure regarding this to This email address is being protected from spambots. You need JavaScript enabled to view it.

Company was very supportive, and a lorry was dispatched that emptied the keel, while crew cleaned and prepared for repairs to be made. At around 2100 hrs, repairs had been conducted and crew topped up cooling liquid, and tested the engine for an hour an half. Samson was reported to company and harbours to be in working condition 2200 hrs
correctiveAction: 19/02/2021 Repairs made on the same day. To be closed out

managementComment: Modtaget. Når årsag / afhjælpning er gjort kan sagen lukkes.

title: Steering
description: During assisting a vessel coming to port, rudders were found to be sticky and/or unresponsive. After several attempts rectifying the issue, the vessel was turned into the desired direction with engines only. Shortly after the rudders responded as appropriate
correctiveAction: After port operations, the rudders were tested extensively, and an ETO was called to investigate. The forward rudder stick control was found to be suffering from wear and tear. After investigating what spares are available, it was decided to switch about the FWD and AFT rudder stick control and order a new to replace the worn one. Further testing was done, however it was impossible to provoke the error to occur again This report can be closed as soon as the stick control has been replaced Rep prepared by EEJ
managementComment: Corrective actions are well accepted. Well done. Please inform ISM department once new parts are arrived and installed. Further testing to be carried out prior port operations to avoid same issues. Testing to be carried out once new parts are arrived and installed. Has the part been replaced??

title: Starting FiFi pump when lying alongside.
description: The FiFi pump was started while we were alongside the quay with PS. The pump revolutions were so high that we washed down the quay and hit an other ship lying alongside 50 to 70 meters away. When operating the FiFi pump with high revolutions when lying in bert, we can draw small stones from the bottom into the pump. This can cause serious damage to people.
correctiveAction: Procedure made for starting the FiFi pump.
managementComment: Corrective action accepted. Please send a proposal for the procedure to HSEQ

title: Sprinklers for windows on bridge
description: We get no water through sprinklers for windows on bridge.
correctiveAction: Coils tested and found to be ok. Voltage for coils tested and found to be ok when we push the button. We continue searching.. We could find out that someone cut the pipe which lead to the bridge. We connected again and tested, and found out that one of the valve were leaking and the other one was not working like it should. Valves were dismantled and it seemed to be the diaphragm that was in bad condition. We have searched for diaphragm for this type of valve, but without luck.

title: Satellite and Magnetic Compass
description: The Satellite Compass does not always work properly. Some days OOW needs to use the tiller, because the Auto Pilot does not always get data from the Satellite Compass. When we on the Auto Pilot shift from Satellite Compass to Magnetic Compass, we get the warning CPS - LO - FAIL
correctiveAction: Connection to instrument for Satelite Compass, and connection to Auto Pilot is tested and found to be ok. The fault seems to be that the Satellite Compass Antenna (disc) is placed too close to the mast. It is only placed 0,5 m from mast, and should at least be 1,5 meters away from a mast with this diameter. We also found out that the coil on the Magnetic Compass is not connected to the Autopilot, and as there is no instruction manual for the Autopilot, which is a Navitron NT921G, we have got info about wiring from Navitron in UK. We will try to find the cable from the Magnetic Compass and connect to the Autopilot. Cable found and magnetic coil connected to Autopilot, so now we can switch between Satellite Compass and Magnetic Compass. Antenna Plan can not be found onboard, so we have started to make an Antenna Plan.

title: Loss of Access to MARAD (PMS)
description: The MARAD Planned Maintenance System (PMS) is currently non-functional on board. The issue has been reported to the DPA, and according to the feedback received, the matter is currently being escalated to the Ship Owner for resolution. As of now, the system remains inaccessible, preventing the technical department from managing maintenance schedules, reporting completed tasks, and monitoring safety-critical equipment intervals.
correctiveAction: 1. To ensure compliance with safety regulations, all mandatory inspections and maintenance tasks (including Safety, Fire-Fighting, and Life-Saving equipment) are being recorded manually in the Deck Log Book and Engine Room Log Book. 2. Critical equipment running hours are tracked via engine room logs to prevent overdue maintenance. 3. Constant communication is maintained with the DPA regarding the status of the system restoration.
finalManagementComment: We constantly reminding owenr to reinstate the MARAD and pay overdues. NC will be closed when MARAD lisense will be renewed.

title: Discrepancies in Garbage Management Plan (GMP)
description: During an internal audit of the vessel's Garbage Management Plan (GMP) and actual garbage collection stations, several significant non-conformities were identified: 1.	Incorrect Location of Main Garbage Station: The current GMP states that garbage receptacles for Plastic, Food, Domestic, and Operational waste are located on the Main Deck, Port Side. However, the actual physical location is on the Poop Deck Level, Starboard Side (Working boat Platform). 2.	Discrepancy in Receptacle Capacities: Actual internal measurements of the containers differ significantly from the volumes stated in the GMP: * Deck Operational Waste (Category F): Actual usable volume is 0.60 m³ (instead of 50L). * Engine Room Bin (Category F): Actual usable volume is 0.07 m³ (instead of 50L). 3.	Inconsistent Color Coding: Category F (Operational Waste) containers have different marking colors in the Engine Room and on Deck, which contradicts the requirement for a unified marking system as per the GMP. 4.	Inapplicable Waste Categories: Category J (Cargo Residues) is currently listed in the inventory but is not applicable to this vessel's type and trade.
correctiveAction: 1. Conducted an internal audit and physical measurements of all garbage receptacles to establish actual usable volumes. 2. Created a 'Marked-up' working copy of the Garbage Management Plan (GMP) with corrected locations and capacities. 3. This corrected page has been printed and inserted into the onboard GMP folder to ensure accurate record-keeping in the Garbage Record Book until an official revision is received from the Company. 4. Briefed the crew/engine department on the corrected capacities for accurate entries.

title: Lifting of heavy items in engine room with expired lifting gear
description: A job involving lifting of Main engines lub oil coolers in engine room to be undertaken by crew upon Superintendents request in Drydock in LIsbon during week 20 and 21. Job includes lifting of plate cooler end piece, chain block inspected before job started and found to be with sufficient safety margin of SWL limits. finalManagementComment: NC accepted. Although lifting gear was inspected and safe to use, certification had expired and Danish regulations were not met. Gear has now been re-certified and passed inspection. Procedures adjusted to ensure timely re-certification in future.

title: Internal audit finding #02
description: During audit we found out that oily water separator not working/not operational
correctiveAction: Even though the vessel is under 400 GT, it is still required to meet the 15 ppm discharge limit, and installing an OWS is the most practical and standard method to ensure compliance. Many flag states may require or recommend an OWS even for vessels under 400 GT, especially if they operate in international waters, to meet these discharge standards. If not we must look into procedures for the treatment of bilge water etc, and specify that all is discharged ashore. Likewise all discharge valves must be closed off with padlocks to ensure that bilge water is not discharged into the sea.
managementComment: Management will take the decision before DEC - 19 / 2024
finalManagementComment: OWS valve sealed and remark was made in ORB.

title: Internal audit finding #01
description: SRC certificate expired
correctiveAction: Ordered technician and class to perform survey

title: NAVTEX
description: NAVTEX can not receive any message. After Radio technician visit on the vessel, he has found that NAVTEX antenna not responding. Recommendation from technician to install new NAVTEX
correctiveAction: Request has been sent to Ship management. New one installed, working properly.
finalManagementComment: New navtex unit was ordered and installed.

title: PSC finding # 9: Shore based maintenence agreement expired.
description: Shore-based maintenance for the radio expired on 07/2023. Root cause: Both management and crew failed to observe the typo on the agreement, and did not see that the expiry date was wrong on the agreement.
correctiveAction: The shore-based maintenance agreement was issued on 10.01.2024, but unfortunately, there is a typo stating that the agreement is expiring on 07.2023, see the attached agreement. A new agreement will be issued with the correct expiry date.
managementComment: This was a typo from the issuers side, should have been noticed by both crew and management. We will take more care when receiveg certificates etc.
finalManagementComment: Closed - but will be followed up by the management.

title: PSC finding # 8: The engine room was found in dirty and unorganized condition and the platform was slippery.
description: The engine room was found in dirty and unorganized condition and the platform was slippery. Root cause: Same as finding # 6, the crew has changed oil on both gensets, and didn't clean the engine room afterwards.
correctiveAction: Engine room was tidyed up, and cleaned overall.
managementComment: Due to the situation onboard the crew has not given enough attetion to the cleanliness of Engine room
finalManagementComment: Closed - but will be monitored by the management.

title: PSC finding # 7: No milk was found onboard.
description: No Milk was found onboard. Master to ensure that there is an adequate quantity of provision onboard for the current voyage. Root cause: The crew orders the provisions themselves, and as they apparently don't drink milk, this was forgotten.
correctiveAction: The crew does not drink milk - therefore no milk were ordered when provisions were ordered. Milk was purchased and brought onboard.
managementComment: The crew normally orders the provisions they want as this is a small vessel.

title: PSC finding # 6: Engine room bilges full of oil.
description: The engine room bilges was full of oil. Root cause: On the previous voyage a false alarm regarding water in the lubricating oil on the genset was received. The crew changed the oil on both gensets as a safety precaution, leading to oil spilled to mhe bilges. Later is was established taht there was a fault on the sensor on the genset, now repaired.
correctiveAction: Oil residues pumped ashore, bilges subsequently cleaned.
managementComment: The crew was instructed to follow procedures, and the arrange foor oil residues to be pumped ashore, in collaboration with the technical manager.
finalManagementComment: CLosed - but will be followed up in the future.

title: PSC finding #5 - Gauge on steering gear wasted.
description: The gauge on the steering gear is in wasted condition. Root cause: This gauge has been wasted for a long period, but was not observed. Crew failed to report to technical management.
correctiveAction: New gauge ordered, and fitted.
managementComment: The crew has failed to report this the to technical manger, so replacement can be done. Crew instructed to be more observant, and report all failures to the management.
finalManagementComment: Closed - but will be followed by the management.

title: PSC finding # 4 - Steering gear alarms
description: The steering gear 1 alarm – Power Failure Steering Gear 1 and Overload Steering Gear 1 are permanently lit on bridge indication. Root cause: Electrical error in breaker/relais, which were not properly handled by the crew.
correctiveAction: An electrician from shore has been onboard to locate the problem and repair this. Was a failure in a relais..
managementComment: The crew has failed to report this error to the technical manager before departure. The crew has been instructed to report all technical faults to the management immidiately.
finalManagementComment: Closed - but will be monitored by the management.

title: PSC finding #3 - Charts not onboard
description: The vessel does not have charts for the current voyage. For example, BA 2902 is missing which is needed for the current location of the vessel. Similarly, IS32 and IS33 which were needed for the current voyage are found missing. Root cause: Due to a hectic program , the crew forgot to pick up deliveries before departure.
correctiveAction: Charts ordered aan d brought onboard.
managementComment: Charts were ordered and delivered to warehouse, but the crew failed to bring these onboard. Crew was instructed to be more careful and follow instructions.
finalManagementComment: Closed, but will be monitored by the management.

title: PSC Finding # 2: Deck area holed and corroded.
description: The starboard anchor foundation area on the main deck was found corroded and holed. Root cause: Maintenance work has been carried out in the area of anchor foundation, but no reporting of corrosion observed in the area has been filed. This is not in compliance with ship ISM system
correctiveAction: The anchor winch was dismantled and new steel plates inserted by a certified welder, all to Class satisfaction, subsequently anchor winch will be re-mounted.
managementComment: These holes was not observed during the stay in shioyard, probably as these were locate under the anchor winch. We wiill check more carefully at annual surveys, and perfom UTM when suspicios areas are observed.
finalManagementComment: The anchor winch was dismantled, all corroded parts of the deck eas removed, and new syteel plates inserted.

title: PSC finding #1: Emergency fire pump - not working.
description: The vessel needs to have 2 fire pumps and 1 emergency fire pump as per the Fire Plan. Only one fire pump was found working onboard at the time of inspection. Root cause:  The emergency fire pump, was not properly washed through with fresh water after last use, leading to corrosion inside. The crew did not check the fire pump before departure
correctiveAction: The emergency fire pump onboard was defective, was replaced with a new one. The crew failed to check the emergency fire pump before departure.
managementComment: The crew was instructed to follow procedures and the check all life-saving and fire-fighting equipment at the required intervals, especially after layup.
finalManagementComment: Closed - but will be followed up by the management.

title: Water ingress to cahin lockers
description: The crew was instructed to apply polyurethane foam to the anchor hawser, but failed to do so, leading to water ingress into chanil lockers. Root cause: the crew did not follow procedures, and instructions, which says that the anchor hawsers must be sealed before proceeding to sea.
correctiveAction: Chain lockers emptied, and crew instructed to follow procedures.
managementComment: The crew must take more care when preparing for sea.
finalManagementComment: Closed, but will be followed up by the management.

title: Water ingress in Bow thuster room
description: The bottom valve oand overboard valve leading to the bowthuster room was not properly closed, causing water to ingrees into the room. Vlaves have been inspected and found in order. Root cause: Due to long layup the valves were not closing correctly, and the crew failed to check.
correctiveAction: Water pumped out and the bowthruster room inspected. All found in good order. A bilge alarm will be installed within shortly.
managementComment: We will implement procedures for a proper check of all valves etc. after layup periods. NC will be closed when Bilge Alarm is installed.

title: NC expired Class certificates
description: The vessel’s ISSC, SMC and MLC certificates expired on 28.05.2026 before the renewal audit could be completed. The renewal audit was originally planned for Fredericia, however due to unexpected water ballast tank damage and urgent repair works, the vessel’s schedule was affected and the audit had to be rescheduled. Class has been contacted, and the renewal audit remains planned for the next suitable port call in Fredericia. The Company will review the case and ensure that contingency options are considered in case of future unexpected operational delays. This NC will be closed after completion of the renewal audit and verification of valid certificates onboard.

title: PSC 26-18408 Paint store
description: Paint store – The ventilation and light were observed out of order.
correctiveAction: The paint store ventilation and lighting were immediately repaired and tested to confirm proper operation. The crew carried out an inspection of the paint store to ensure the space is safe for use, properly ventilated, and adequately illuminated.

title: PSC 26-18401 Medical Equipement
description: Medical Equipment – the resuscitator onboard found not assembled and ready to use.
correctiveAction: The resusciator was immediately checked, fully assembled, and arranged in a ready-to-use condition.

title: PSC 26-18408 Electrical box
description: Electrical box for the communication in the steering gear room found without cover.
correctiveAction: The missing cover for the electrical communication box in the steering gear room was immediately fitted/replaced to ensure the box is properly protected and safe for operation. A full inspection of electrical boxes, panels, and communication equipment in the steering gear room and other relevant areas was carried out to verify that all covers are in place, properly secured, and in good condition. The crew has been instructed to include visual checks of electrical boxes and panel covers during routine inspections, and any missing or damaged covers shall be reported and rectified immediately.

title: Emergency escape hatch from the engine room was found not operational, affecting safe emergency evacuation.
description: Emergency escape routes and exits, including engine room emergency escape hatch, shall be maintained in operational condition at all times to ensure safe evacuation in case of emergency.
correctiveAction: The defective emergency hatch was repaired and tested to ensure full operational condition. Inspection and testing of all emergency escape routes were carried out. PMS updated if required.
finalManagementComment: We acknowledge the seriousness of the finding, as emergency escape routes must remain fully operational at all times. The immediate corrective action taken on board is considered appropriate. The emergency escape hatch was repaired and tested, and additional checks of all emergency escape routes were carried out to verify their operational condition. We will add routine checks of emergency exits into PMS to prevent reocurrance.

title: REC 01/2026 - Internal Audit
description: It is recommended to consider a more user-friendly system for recording rest hours, subject to Flag approval.

title: OBS 05/2026 - Internal Audit
description: No record of the test of the vessel cargo hoses was produced.

title: OBS 04/2026 - Internal Audit
description: Bunker pipeline not pressure tested as required.

title: OBS 03/2026 - Internal Audit
description: CE’s standing orders found in the dressing room are not readable.
correctiveAction: The Chief Engineer will replace the unreadable standing orders with a clear and legible copy. The document will be properly displayed and maintained in good condition.
finalManagementComment: Uploaded into SMS and posted on the wall.

title: OBS 02/2026 - Internal Audit
description: ODME inoperative, short-term IOPP issued.
correctiveAction: Class is informed and ST IOPP issued.

title: OBS 01/2026 - Internal Audit
description: It was observed during spot checks on board that not all deliveries were accompanied by an MSDS, as required under the IHM maintenance procedure.
correctiveAction: The management and vessel’s Master will reinforce the requirement that all deliveries must be accompanied by a valid MSDS in accordance with the procedure 10.9.0 of the SMS. The crew will be instructed to verify the presence of MSDS upon receipt of goods, and any missing documentation will be requested from the supplier before acceptance or use. Records will be maintained on board, and compliance will be monitored through periodic checks and internal audits.

title: NC 02/2026 - Internal Audit
description: No objective evidence of the use of a specific cargo plan was identified during the audit, nor was a procedure for its preparation found.
correctiveAction: The management will develop and implement a procedure for the preparation and use of cargo plans. The bridge team will be instructed to prepare and retain a documented cargo plan for each operation, approved by the Master. Compliance will be verified during internal audits.
finalManagementComment: We have implemented cargo plan checklists 7-0507I LOADING DISCHARGING BALLAST PLANNING

title: NC 01/2026 - Internal Audit
description: During the SMS review, no objective evidence was found that a risk assessment system was in place.
correctiveAction: The Master has been instructed to rectify this within one month.

title: Near miss Helmsman seat
description: The bridge helmsman seat has suffered significant structural failure. The chair's internal support mechanism is broken, causing it to tilt at critical, unstable angle Furthermore, the external upholstery is torn, exposing  sharp metal internal structures.
correctiveAction: It is recommended to supply a heavy-duty, marine-grade replacement seat immediately.
finalManagementComment: To be delegated to the ship`s superintendent.

title: NC 03-26 Tank Monitoring System
description: NC 03-26 Tank Monitoring System.
correctiveAction: Download 970nc0326tankmonitoringsystem.docx Type: application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document Size: 0.12 MB Hits: 6
managementComment: Independent UTI measurements shall be used at all times to verify tank level readings during cargo operations, particularly when approaching critical filling limits, to ensure safe control and prevent overfilling. The crew must check between the Tank Monitoring System indications and manual measurements during cargo loading. Crew should identify the reasons for the incorrect measurements and decide with technical superintendent how to rectify it. Until permanent rectification is completed, manual verification remains a compulsory control measure for all cargo operations.

title: NC 02-26 SUZUKI DF30A
description: NC 02-26 SUZUKI DF30A
correctiveAction: Download nc0226suzukidf30a.docx Type: application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document Size: 0.77 MB Hits: 4
finalManagementComment: SOLAS/LSA Code does not explicitly require Teleflex steering. It does not require remote throttle/gear control specifically. It requires the boat to be safe, controllable, and capable of maneuvering effectively Superindendent is responsible to check with owners how they want the boat steering to be made.

title: AB not wearing a PPE
description: During routine operations on deck, AB was found observed working without wearing a safety helmet. No injury occured, however the situation had the potential to result in serious head injury if object had fallen or the seaman had slipped.
correctiveAction: Safety briefing completed with deck crew. Reminder issued on mandatory use of PPE at all times on deck. Increased supervision by duty officer. PPE compliance to be monitored during toolbox talks. PPE must be worn at all times in designated areas. Even short-term NC can lead to serious injury. Conduct PPE compliance inspections by officers and supervisors.
finalManagementComment: Management has reviewed the near-miss involving a crew member working on deck without a safety helmet. Although no injury occurred, the situation had the potential to result in serious harm. Immediate corrective actions were taken, including a safety briefing, reinforcement of mandatory PPE requirements, increased supervision, and confirmation that this near-miss will be referred to during upcoming toolbox talks and safety meetings. If near-miss reocurs, verbal warnings may be issued to the crew. The near-miss is considered closed.

title: ER Hot Water Heaters
description: ER Hot Water El. Heaters (15kW and 30kW) which works in auto mode and as usually work when ME stopped. So this heaters have 3 fuses in MSB for safety reason. This fuses were found in very hot condition, so hot that plastic plate around fuses became black due to overheating
correctiveAction: Hazardous situation (fire) could have occurred in ER. The fuses were immidiately replaced for new one's 63A each. But situation repeated. We have switched off heaters for the night time, next day we have tried to switch off only one heater, then another fuse became warm and not hot. We have rechecked with shore electrician el.scheme and made measurements. His decision: two heaters give 60A current, but fuses have 63A or almost max current, that is why fuses heats. Need work only with one heater for safety or replace cable from MSB with fuses for more powerfull (about 80A)
finalManagementComment: Management has reviewed the near-miss involving overheating of fuses supplying the ER hot water electric heaters. The investigation confirmed that when both heaters operate simultaneously, the combined load is close to the rated capacity of the installed 63A fuses, leading to overheating and increased fire risk. As an immediate risk-control measure, operation has been limited to one heater only. Electrical schematics and load measurements were reviewed together with a shore electrician, confirming that the existing cable and fuse rating are marginal for simultaneous operation. Permanent corrective action will be evaluated, including upgrading the supply cables and protective devices to an appropriate rating in accordance with load requirements. The near-miss has been addressed, risk mitigated, and the case is considered closed, subject to implementation of the permanent corrective action.

title: Free-Fall Lifeboat Davit Rollers
description: During inspection of the FFLB davit, it was found that the rollers pins on which the rope runs are not equipped with grease nipples. The roller pins have no lubrication fittings, which makes it impossible to apply grease in a proper and controlled manner. This condition may lead to insufficient lubrication, increased wear, and potential malfunction of the roller. Roller pins on the FFLB davit were supplied without lubrication fittings (grease nipples) as part of the original equipment design.
correctiveAction: The installation of the pin without a lubrication grease nipple on the FFLB davit is in accordance with the manufacturer’s design. This has been verified and confirmed. Accordingly, non-conformity can be considered closed
finalManagementComment: Management has reviewed the non-conformance and confirmed that the FFLB davit roller pins were supplied without grease nipples in accordance with the manufacturer’s approved original design. The arrangement complies with the manufacturer’s specifications and does not represent a deviation. No corrective action is required, and the non-conformance is considered closed.
